description: This SAS code processes sales data to create a new dataset with trend analysis. It uses RETAIN to maintain running totals and implement lag patterns, enabling calculations of moving averages and percentage changes between consecutive sales. The code handles multiple product IDs, resetting calculations for each new product group. It includes data validation checks and flags significant changes in sales patterns. Key RETAIN-specific constructs include the RETAIN statement, LAG function, and conditional processing based on FIRST.variable patterns.

description: This SAS code uses the RETAIN statement to calculate various business metrics from sales data. It processes a raw sales dataset to compute running totals, averages, month-over-month changes, and quarterly performance indicators. Key RETAIN constructs include: tracking cumulative values (RunningTotal), counting observations (MonthCount), calculating running averages, and maintaining state variables (FirstObsFlag). The code demonstrates several sophisticated patterns like multi-period aggregates (quarterly metrics) and time series comparisons while maintaining application state between observations.
